data.title: Analyst Reiterates Hold Rating and Maintains $150 Price Target Amid Conservative Outlook and Balanced Risk-Reward
data.description:
Analyst Juan C. Sanabria of BMO Capital maintained a Hold rating on Extra Space Storage, retaining the price target of $150.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Juan C. Sanabria has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a modest earnings beat in the first quarter and management’s decision to keep full‑year guidance unchanged and still slightly below consensus. While core FFO outperformed expectations and in‑place rent growth showed improvement, management appears reluctant to raise forecasts before seeing how demand develops through the peak leasing season amid a choppy macro backdrop.

Sanabria views the company’s stance as prudently conservative, noting that April and subsequent leasing trends will be important to justify any upward revision to estimates. With only incremental operational improvement, limited new investment activity, and guidance that already embeds cautious assumptions, he sees a balanced risk‑reward profile at current levels, supporting a Hold rating and an unchanged $150 price target.

In another report released on April 13, Evercore ISI also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $149.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Xylem After Q1 Beat, Raised Revenue Outlook, and Strong Water Metering Momentum
data.description:
William Blair analyst Brian Drab has maintained their bullish stance on XYL stock, giving a Buy rating on April 20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Brian Drab has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Xylem’s recent performance and outlook. The company slightly exceeded first-quarter consensus expectations, kept its earnings and margin guidance intact, and even nudged revenue guidance higher to reflect the anticipated closing of its international metering asset sale.

Moreover, Xylem delivered better-than-feared results in its water metering segment, posting modest organic revenue growth instead of the projected decline and generating a 15% increase in orders within measurement and control solutions. Management also reaffirmed a long-term high-single-digit growth outlook for this segment and signaled that water metering orders should rise at a double-digit pace for the rest of the year, supporting confidence in sustained growth and justifying the Buy recommendation.

Drab covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Thermon Group Holdings, nVent Electric, and Vertiv Holdings. According to TipRanks, Drab has an average return of 32.2% and a 73.87% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 20, Goldman Sachs also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $155.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Spotify as AI-Linked Costs Rise and Strategic Risks Prompt Price Target Cut to $400
data.description:
Pivotal Research analyst Jeffrey Wlodarczak reiterated a Hold rating on Spotify today and set a price target of $400.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jeffrey Wlodarczak has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, starting with the view that Spotify’s recent quarter was broadly solid and roughly matched his expectations, but its near-term guidance implies meaningfully higher spending tied to AI, marketing, and royalty obligations. Those higher projected costs, along with a weaker expense outlook, led him to reduce his year-end 2026 price target from $420 to $400, leaving the shares trading close to what he sees as fair value.

At the same time, he sees growing strategic risk from AI, which could erode Spotify’s key advantages in premium streaming and recommendations, encourage more competition for listening time, and potentially compress the long-term opportunity in paid music. While he acknowledges Spotify is actively deploying AI tools like its AI DJ and other personalization features, he doubts these initiatives will fully offset the structural pressures, including likely future price hikes and rising royalty demands, so he believes a neutral stance remains appropriate.

data.title: Cautious Stance Maintained as Chloe Lemarie Cuts Price Target to $185 on Constrained Upside Potential
data.description:
Analyst Chloe Lemarie from Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on Airbus Group SE and decreased the price target to €185.00 from €190.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Chloe Lemarie has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, notably that Q1 operating profit in the commercial segment fell short of market expectations, even though this was partly cushioned by a better-than-anticipated performance in Defense & Security. Free cash flow was also pressured by higher inventory levels, which tempers the near-term financial picture despite some constructive signals from management.

At the same time, Lemarie believes the stock’s risk/reward profile is not compelling enough to justify a more positive stance, particularly given ongoing doubts about the durability of air traffic trends and the still modest pace of delivery ramp-up early in Q2. Reflecting this more cautious balance, the official price target was trimmed from $190 to $185, reinforcing the view that upside potential remains constrained for now.

According to TipRanks, Lemarie is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 26.8% and a 69.96% success rate. Lemarie covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Airbus Group SE, RENK Group AG, and BAE Systems.

In another report released on April 16, Kepler Capital also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a €196.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Humacyte, Citing Hemodialysis Upside and KOL Support, Keeps $1 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Humacyte, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Josh Jennings from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $1.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Josh Jennings has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors highlighted during Humacyte’s recent KOL call, which underscored ATEV’s potential to address unmet needs in arteriovenous access for hemodialysis. He views the favorable expert feedback on ATEV’s clinical and economic benefits, along with the encouraging female subgroup data from the V007 Phase III study, as reinforcing the long-term value of the platform beyond its current trauma indication.

Jennings also points to the large end-stage renal disease market and the ongoing V012 Phase III trial in female hemodialysis patients, with interim data expected in 2Q26, as key upside drivers. While recognizing that Symvess’s extremity vascular trauma use is the primary near-term growth engine, he believes the broader hemodialysis opportunity provides significant expansion potential, supporting his Buy rating and unchanged $1 price target.

According to TipRanks, Jennings is a 3-star analyst with an average return of 0.9% and a 44.09% success rate. Jennings covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Boston Scientific, TransMedics Group, and Abbott Laboratories.

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Vertex as Cost-Cutting, AI Focus Boost FCF Outlook; Price Target Maintained at $20
data.description:

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Joshua Reilly has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Vertex’s new “Value Creation Plan” and improving financial profile. The company is sharpening its focus on becoming more AI-enabled and streamlining operations, including a 9% headcount reduction, which he expects will remove prior inefficiencies and better align costs with growth.

Reilly estimates these changes should lift annual free cash flow from about $70 million to roughly $90 million, even after accounting for one-time restructuring charges in FY26. With margins set to move closer to peers and a more decisive pace of change under new CEO Chris Young, he anticipates the stock will respond positively, supporting his reiterated Buy rating and unchanged $20 price target.

data.title: Commvault Maintained at Hold as Strong Q4 Performance Offsets Cautious Outlook and Slower Growth Guidance
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jason Ader has maintained their neutral stance on CVLT stock, giving a Hold rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jason Ader has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both solid execution and a more cautious outlook. He acknowledges that Commvault just delivered a strong fourth quarter, with double‑digit revenue growth, accelerating subscription and SaaS ARR, record free cash flow, and better‑than‑expected profitability, all of which signal improved execution and growing customer adoption.

At the same time, he points out that management’s guidance implies a notable slowdown in ARR and revenue growth versus the prior year, and that overall revenue is projected to fall short of prior Street expectations. The mix shift toward SaaS, while strategically attractive, is also creating near‑term top‑line headwinds, which, combined with the decelerating growth profile, limits upside potential for the shares in his view and supports maintaining a neutral stance for now.

In another report released yesterday, Jefferies also initiated coverage with a Hold rating on the stock with a $105.00 price target.

data.title: Cullinan Oncology: FDA Acceptance of Zipalertinib NDA and Upcoming CLN-978 Data Underpin Buy Rating and Favorable NPV Outlook
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Phipps has maintained their bullish stance on CGEM stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Matt Phipps has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including the recent FDA acceptance of the NDA for zipalertinib in EGFR exon 20 insertion–mutated NSCLC, which establishes a clear regulatory path with a defined PDUFA date in early 2027. While not the primary driver of the story, he views the Taiho partnership and forthcoming REZILIENT3 Phase III data as creating incremental upside in both second-line and potentially first-line settings.

He also emphasizes Cullinan’s nearer-term catalyst from the upcoming dose-escalation data for CLN-978 at EULAR, which could meaningfully reshape investor perception of the pipeline. With zipalertinib’s differentiated efficacy and tolerability profile supporting use post-chemotherapy/Rybrevant and enabling chemo combinations upfront, alongside largely unchanged peak sales assumptions and updated launch timelines, Phipps’s model supports a favorable net-present value that underpins his Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $30.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on Zimmer Biomet, Keeps $109 Price Target Amid Ongoing Multi-Year Transformation
data.description:
Mathew Blackman, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on Zimmer Biomet Holdings. The associated price target remains the same with $109.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mathew Blackman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Zimmer Biomet’s multi‑year transformation and early execution against its 2026 objectives. He views 2026 as a rebuilding year focused on setting achievable targets, restoring management credibility, and executing consistently, and notes that the company has already modestly exceeded expectations across key metrics despite headwinds such as U.S. knee SKU rationalization and a difficult first‑quarter comparison.

Blackman also highlights a compelling longer‑term trajectory, with 2027 expected to benefit from a series of differentiated product launches and a more focused commercial footprint, which he believes can drive a gradual grind higher in earnings off a discounted valuation. Looking beyond 2028, his thesis assumes Zimmer Biomet can sustain mid‑single‑digit revenue growth, expand gross margins toward 70%, and lift EBITDA into the mid‑30% range, turning the business into a strong cash generator, and he maintains his $109 price target unchanged as these elements remain intact.

Blackman covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Intuitive Surgical, and Beta Bionics, Inc.. According to TipRanks, Blackman has an average return of 8.8% and a 44.59% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 13, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $101.00 price target.
